title: Restrict --strict to high-confidence, direct, non-stale invariants
context: Previously strict mode failed on ANY blocking invariant in scope, including blast-radius guesses and stale records. On shared repos, false positives could wrongly block teammates' commits. Touches invariant(s): con_8460b6770f (Config/provider writers must merge idempotently into existing user files and refuse to clobber an unparseable file).
decision: Extract hardened gate (strictgate.ts) that permits blocking ONLY on: directly-scoped, high-confidence (≥0.8), non-stale blocking invariants OR human-confirmed sources. All weaker blocking hits downgrade to advisory. Centralizes the rule in one audited, unit-tested module mirroring hookpolicy.ts.
consequences: Blast-radius ('near') hits never block, only surface as review items
Stale or low-confidence auto-derived records never block
Safe to enable strict mode in shared team pre-commit hooks
Human-confirmed invariants always block regardless of numeric confidence
Weaker signals still print (advisory) so nothing silent gets dropped
alternatives_rejected: Fix specific false positives case-by-case (doesn't address root: unsafe semantics)
Improve staleness detection instead (still unsafe if detection has gaps)
Separate 'team-safe' flag variant (feature duplication, configuration burden)
